title: Cognitive Load Assessment Survey
description: This survey assesses the degree to which your mind is cluttered with excessive information and minor tasks, based on Cognitive Load Theory (Krieglstein et al., 2022).
source: Inspired by: Krieglstein, F., Beege, M., Rey, G. D., Ginns, P., Krell, M., & Schneider, S. (2022). A systematic meta-analysis of the reliability and validity of subjective cognitive load questionnaires in experimental multimedia learning research. Educational Psychology Review, 34, 2485–2541. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10648-022-09683-4

questions.text: How mentally demanding do you find it to keep track of all your current responsibilities at once?

options.label: Not demanding at all — everything feels manageable
Slightly demanding
Moderately demanding
Very demanding — it takes significant effort
Extremely demanding — I feel overwhelmed

questions.text: How much do interruptions (calls, messages, noise) disrupt your ability to think clearly during the day?

options.label: Not at all — I filter them out easily
Slightly — minor disruption only
Moderately — I lose my train of thought
Significantly — it takes time to refocus
Completely — interruptions constantly derail me

questions.text: How complex does it feel to mentally juggle unfinished tasks alongside new incoming ones?

options.label: Not complex — I handle it naturally
Slightly complex
Moderately complex
Very complex — it requires real effort
Extremely complex — I cannot manage it

questions.text: How often does switching between different apps, platforms, or tools during work increase your mental confusion?

options.label: Never — switching feels seamless
Occasionally
Fairly often
Most of the time
Always — it significantly fragments my thinking

questions.text: When you learn something new, how much mental effort do you invest in connecting it to what you already know?

options.label: A great deal — I actively build connections
Quite a bit
Some effort
Very little effort
None — I absorb information passively without connecting it

questions.text: How difficult is it for you to hold multiple pieces of important information in mind without writing them down?

options.label: Not difficult — I retain information easily
Slightly difficult
Moderately difficult
Very difficult — I frequently lose track
Extremely difficult — I rely heavily on external reminders

questions.text: How much does a disorganised physical or digital workspace increase the effort required to complete tasks?

options.label: Not at all — environment does not affect me
Slightly
Noticeably — I waste time searching or refocusing
Significantly — it causes regular frustration
Enormously — I cannot function in disorder

questions.text: After completing a difficult task, how often do you reflect on what you have learned from it?

options.label: Almost always — reflection is a habit
Often
Sometimes
Rarely
Almost never — I move straight to the next task

questions.text: How much mental effort does it take to understand complex instructions or multi-step directions at first read?

options.label: Very little — I grasp them immediately
Some effort — I read carefully once
Moderate effort — I reread parts
Considerable effort — I need several readings
Enormous effort — complex instructions overwhelm me

questions.text: How often do redundant or contradictory pieces of information (e.g., conflicting advice online) increase your mental fatigue?

options.label: Almost never — I identify what is relevant quickly
Occasionally
Fairly often
Most of the time
Always — conflicting information leaves me mentally drained

questions.text: When tackling a familiar type of problem, how automatically do you apply previously learned strategies?

options.label: Very automatically — prior strategies come naturally
Mostly automatically with minor thought
Partially — I still need to consciously recall steps
With difficulty — strategies feel distant
Not at all — I approach every problem from scratch

questions.text: How effortful is it for you to mentally organise a large amount of incoming information into a coherent picture?

options.label: Not effortful — I structure information intuitively
Slightly effortful
Moderately effortful
Very effortful — structuring information drains me
Extremely effortful — I feel lost in information

questions.text: How much does receiving too many notifications throughout the day interfere with your ability to think deeply?

options.label: Not at all — I have full control over notifications
Slightly
Noticeably — deep thinking becomes harder
Significantly — sustained focus feels impossible
Completely — notifications constantly fracture my concentration

questions.text: How often do you actively try to simplify or reorganise information to make it easier to remember?

options.label: Very often — simplification is part of how I think
Often
Sometimes
Rarely
Almost never — I store information as I receive it

questions.text: How much mental effort is required for you to switch your focus between two very different topics in a short time?

options.label: Very little — I switch contexts fluidly
Some effort
Moderate effort — I need a moment to adjust
Considerable effort — switching is mentally costly
Enormous effort — context switching exhausts me

questions.text: How often does the volume of daily information (news, emails, social media) make it harder to focus on what truly matters?

options.label: Almost never — I filter information effectively
Occasionally
Fairly often
Most days
Every day — information overload is constant

questions.text: How well do you feel you understand the underlying logic of tasks you work on, rather than just following steps mechanically?

options.label: Very well — I always seek to understand the 'why'
Mostly well
Partially — sometimes I just follow steps
Rarely — I mostly follow procedures without deeper understanding
Almost never — I focus only on completing steps

questions.text: How demanding do you find it to keep a clear overview of a project that involves many interdependent steps?

options.label: Not demanding — I maintain a clear mental map
Slightly demanding
Moderately demanding
Very demanding — details blur together
Extremely demanding — I lose the overview completely

questions.text: How much does poorly presented or cluttered information (e.g., messy reports, chaotic emails) increase your mental effort?

options.label: Not at all — I extract what I need regardless
Slightly
Noticeably — poor presentation slows me down
Significantly — it causes real frustration
Enormously — cluttered information paralyses me

questions.text: How often do you notice yourself developing better mental strategies or shortcuts after repeated exposure to similar tasks?

options.label: Very often — I constantly refine my approach
Often
Sometimes
Rarely
Almost never — each similar task still feels equally hard

scoring.description: Your working memory operates with excellent efficiency. Information is processed and organised with ease. No intervention required.
You experience mild mental clutter. Simple habits such as task lists and brief digital detoxes will help maintain your clarity.
Noticeable cognitive burden is beginning to affect your focus and memory. Structured information management and regular mental breaks are recommended.
Your working memory is significantly strained by excessive information and tasks. Deliberate workload reduction and mindfulness practices are strongly advised.
You are carrying an unsustainable cognitive burden. Professional guidance in cognitive and stress management is recommended.
Your cognitive resources are critically depleted. Immediate lifestyle changes and support from a psychologist or counsellor are urgently needed.
You are experiencing a cognitive crisis. Please seek immediate support from a qualified mental health professional.
